varieties.name: Calypso
varieties.origin: British breeding program, developed for bolt resistance
varieties.traits: Compact, stocky, low-growing habit; full bulky plants with abundant leaves; 12-18 inches tall and wide
varieties.yield_notes: High leaf yield; regrows 3+ times after cutting; ready in 50-65 days

varieties.name: Santo (Long Standing Santo)
varieties.origin: Developed as industry standard slow-bolt variety
varieties.traits: Vigorous growth; strong, upright stems; 1-2 feet tall; uniform plant habit
varieties.yield_notes: High leaf production; seeds ready in 90-95 days
varieties.availability: Available from Australian seed suppliers, Botanical Interests, multiple garden centers

varieties.name: Leisure (Leafy Leisure)
varieties.origin: Santo-type variety bred for commercial and home gardens
varieties.traits: Vigorous, bushy growth; uniform habit; light feathery green foliage; heat tolerant; 20 inches tall
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding; ready for harvest in 28-40 days
varieties.availability: Available from Everwilde Farms, Australian suppliers, widely distributed

varieties.name: Cruiser
varieties.origin: Hybrid variety developed for commercial market
varieties.traits: Compact, tidier, more upright than Calypso; uniform fancy appearance; 7 inches tall

varieties.name: Slow Bolt (Slow Bolting)
varieties.traits: Strong, pungent character; semi-erect to erect growth; 60 cm tall
varieties.yield_notes: Good leaf production before bolting; ready 6-7 weeks

varieties.name: Caribe
varieties.yield_notes: Abundance of leaves; matures 40-55 days; extended harvest period

varieties.name: Standard Coriander (Coriandrum sativum)
varieties.origin: Traditional species type, widely grown worldwide
varieties.traits: Variable growth habit; 30-60 cm tall; traditional characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate; quick to bolt in heat; leaves ready 30-45 days; seeds 90-110 days

varieties.name: Mexican Coriander (Culantro, Vietnamese Coriander)
varieties.origin: Native to Mexico, Caribbean, Central and South America; naturalized in Southeast Asia
varieties.traits: Biennial/short-lived perennial; rosette growth form; long serrated leaves (5 cm wide, 30 cm tall); sawtooth edges
varieties.yield_notes: Continuous harvest for 1-2 years in warm climates; 10-12 weeks to first harvest
varieties.availability: Succeed Heirlooms Australia (as Mexican Coriander), Vietnamese specialty nurseries, online seed suppliers

varieties.name: Cleo
varieties.traits: High-performing variety specifically bred for warmer conditions

varieties.name: Confetti Leaf
varieties.traits: Attractive fern-like foliage; fine, delicate appearance; ornamental value
varieties.yield_notes: Good for microgreens and baby leaf harvest
varieties.availability: Johnny's Selected Seeds, specialty herb suppliers

varieties.name: Delfino
varieties.traits: Ultra-fine, feathery foliage throughout plant; unique appearance
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yield; prized for unique texture

varieties.name: Swathi
varieties.origin: Guntur, Andhra Pradesh, India - developed for South Indian conditions
varieties.traits: Semi-erect growing habit; medium height; good branching
varieties.availability: Widely available in Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Kerala state agricultural departments, local seed suppliers

varieties.name: Sindhu
varieties.traits: Dwarf variety with compact growth; good for high-density planting
varieties.availability: Kerala agricultural universities, Andhra Pradesh seed suppliers, South Indian seed companies

varieties.name: Sadhana
varieties.traits: Medium-tall variety with semi-erect stems; bushy nature good for dual purpose

varieties.name: Rajendra Swathi (RD-44)
varieties.origin: Dholi, Bihar, India - developed for northern and eastern India
varieties.traits: Medium height variety with fine growth characteristics
varieties.availability: Bihar agricultural universities, North Indian seed suppliers, can be sourced through Indian agricultural networks

varieties.name: CO-1
varieties.origin: Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, India - developed by Tamil Nadu Agricultural University
varieties.traits: Dwarf variety with compact growth habit
varieties.availability: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University, South Indian seed networks, available in Kerala through agricultural stores

varieties.name: CO-2
varieties.yield_notes: Grain: 5.2 quintals/hectare; Green leaves: 100 quintals/hectare; matures in 90-110 days
